devanagari: वर्णधर्मनिरूपणम् आश्रमेषु ब्रह्मचारी गृहस्थ धर्मवर्णं च । श्रीउद्धव उवाच । यस्त्वयाभिहितः पूर्वं धर्मस्त्वद्‍भक्तिलक्षणः । वर्णाश्रमाचा...
english_devnagari: varṇadharmanirūpaṇam āśrameṣu brahmacārī gṛhastha dharmavarṇaṃ ca śrīuddhava uvāca yastvayābhihitaḥ pūrvaṃ dharmastvad‍bhaktilakṣaṇaḥ varṇāśramācāravatāṃ sarveṣāṃ dvipadāmapi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[accusative single from √ van class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ van class 8 verb]

translation:
Uddhava said:
Formerly (in the previous Kalpa), You have proclaimed the righteous course (Dharma) which was chiefly characterised by Devotion to you. It was conducive to the well-being of persons eligible to follow (the duties prescribed by Vedas) for the (four) classes of Society (varṇas) and the (four) stages of life (Āśramas), but also to all human beings in general.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
dvipadāmapi—This implies that men who do not belong to any varṇa or āśrama (are beyond the pale of Hinduism) may be benefited by developing devotion to the Supreme Self—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: यथानुष्ठीयमानेन त्वयि भक्तिर्नृणां भवेत् । स्वधर्मेणारविन्दाक्ष तत् समाख्यातुमर्हसि ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: yathānuṣṭhīyamānena tvayi bhaktirnṛṇāṃ bhavet svadharmeṇāravindākṣa tat samākhyātumarhasi
translation: O Lotus-eyed Lord! It behoves you to kindly explain in details the prescribed course of duties (ordained for everyone) by observing which Devotion (Bhakti) maybe developed in men.

devanagari: पुरा किल महाबाहो धर्मं परमकं प्रभो । यत्तेन हंसरूपेण ब्राह्मणेऽभ्यात्थ माधव ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: purā kila mahābāho dharmaṃ paramakaṃ prabho yattena haṃsarūpeṇa brāhmaṇe
translation: O mighty-armed Lord of Lakṣmī! It is reported that in times of yore, you assumed that form of a divine swan[2] and taught this righteous course of a supreme and blissful nature to god Brahmā, O Lord.

devanagari: स इदानीं सुमहता कालेनामित्रकर्शन । न प्रायो भविता मर्त्य लोके प्राक् अनुशासितः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa idānīṃ sumahatā kālenāmitrakarśana na prāyo bhavitā martya loke prāk anuśāsitaḥ
translation: But, as a pretty long time has elapsed since it was proclaimed by you, it does not at present survive (in human memory) in this mortal world, O slayer of enemies.

devanagari: वक्ता कर्ताविता नान्यो धर्मस्याच्युत ते भुवि । सभायामपि वैरिञ्च्यां यत्र मूर्तिधराः कलाः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: vaktā kartāvitā nānyo dharmasyācyuta te bhuvi sabhāyāmapi vairiñcyāṃ yatra mūrtidharāḥ kalāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: There is no person other than you who can propound, practise or defend the Dharma in this world, O immortal Lord. Nor was there any in the assembly of god Brahmā which (the presiding deities of) arts and lores attend in a personal form.

devanagari: कर्त्रावित्रा प्रवक्त्रा च भवता मधुसूदन । त्यक्ते महीतले देव विनष्टं कः प्रवक्ष्यति ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: kartrāvitrā pravaktrā ca bhavatā madhusūdana tyakte mahītale deva vinaṣṭaṃ kaḥ pravakṣyati

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [locative single from √ tyaj class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ tyaj class 1 verb]

translation: O Slayer of Madhu! When this terrestrial globe is left by you who are the founder, protector and expounder (of this Dharma) who is there to teach this (practically) inexistent course of righteousness, O Lord?

devanagari: तत्त्वं नः सर्वधर्मज्ञ धर्मः त्वद्‍भक्तिलक्षणः । यथा यस्य विधीयेत तथा वर्णय मे प्रभो ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tattvaṃ naḥ sarvadharmajña dharmaḥ tvad‍bhaktilakṣaṇaḥ yathā yasya vidhīyeta tathā varṇaya me prabho
translation: Therefore, O Lord who know all the dharmas, be pleased to expound to me the righteous course characterised by (and leading to) devotion to you, its nature and its procedure as advisedly prescribed- to each of us.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्थं स्वभृत्यमुख्येन पृष्टः स भगवान् हरिः । प्रीतः क्षेमाय मर्त्यानां धर्मान् आह सनातनान् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca itthaṃ svabhṛtyamukhyena pṛṣṭaḥ sa bhagavān hariḥ prītaḥ kṣemāya martyānāṃ dharmān āha sanātanān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Thus solicited by the most prominent of his servants (devotee), the glorious Lord Hari was highly pleased and propounded the eternal course of righteous duties conducive to the welfare of mortal beings.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । धर्म्य एष तव प्रश्नो नैःश्रेयसकरो नृणाम् । वर्णाश्रमाचारवतां तमुद्धव निबोध मे ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca dharmya eṣa tava praśno naiḥśreyasakaro nṛṇām varṇāśramācāravatāṃ tamuddhava nibodha me

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[accusative single from √ van class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ van class 8 verb]

translation:
The Lord said:
Your query is intimately concerned with and conducive to dharma, inasmuch as it leads to the highest good of men who observe the duties prescribed for (their respective) Āśrama (stage of life) and Varṇa (class in society). Please listen to its exposition, O Uddhava.

devanagari: आदौ कृतयुगे वर्णो नृणां हंस इति स्मृतः । कृतकृत्याः प्रजा जात्या तस्मात् कृतयुगं विदुः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: ādau kṛtayuge varṇo nṛṇāṃ haṃsa iti smṛtaḥ kṛtakṛtyāḥ prajā jātyā tasmāt kṛtayugaṃ viduḥ
translation: In the beginning (of this Kalpa), in the Kṛta Age, there was only one class among men[3] and that class was known as Haṃsa. By their very Birth, people accomplished all their objects in life (by propitiating me and doing nothing else except my devotion). Hence that age came to be known as Kṛta.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
EK: The society was class-less as there was no division of men in four classes. No recitation of Vedas of different brandies and no Karma; were prescribed for observance. The one-class society was called Haṃsa as all men were always engaged in the uninterrupted japa of so'ham (their identity with Brahman)—EB. 17.63-64.

devanagari: वेदः प्रणव एवाग्रे धर्मोऽहं वृषरूपधृक् । उपासते तपोनिष्ठा हंसं मां मुक्तकिल्बिषाः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: vedaḥ praṇava evāgre dharmo upāsate taponiṣṭhā haṃsaṃ māṃ muktakilbiṣāḥ
translation: In that first Age, Praṇava i.e. Oṃ constituted the (entire compilation) of the Vedic text (which thus contained no prescriptions and interdictions).

devanagari: त्रेतामुखे महाभाग प्राणान् मे हृदयात् त्रयी । विद्या प्रादुरभूत् तस्या अहमासं त्रिवृन्मखः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tretāmukhe mahābhāga prāṇān me hṛdayāt trayī vidyā prādurabhūt tasyā ahamāsaṃ trivṛnmakhaḥ
translation: At the beginning of the Tretā Age (after the Kṛta- yuga) the three-fold Vedic lore (consisting of the Ṛgveda, Sāmaveda and Yajurveda) was manifested from my heart through my breath, O blessed Uddhava. Out of that trinity of Vedas, I (who am of the nature of sacrifice) became of threefold (sacrificial) functions (viz. those of Hotṛ, Udgātṛ and Adhvaryu).

devanagari: विप्रक्षत्रियविट्शूद्रा मुखबाहूरुपादजाः । वैराजात् पुरुषात् जाता य आत्माचारलक्षणाः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: viprakṣatriyaviṭśūdrā mukhabāhūrupādajāḥ vairājāt puruṣāt jātā ya ātmācāralakṣaṇāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 4 verb]

[nominative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation: From the Person of the cosmic Man, the classes of society (varṇas) viz. Brāhmaṇa, Kṣattriya, Vaiśya and Śūdra sprang (respectively) from the mouth, arms, thighs and feet (of the Cosmic Person). And they were severally characterised by their own righteous duties and conduct.

devanagari: गृहाश्रमो जघनतो ब्रह्मचर्यं हृदो मम । वक्षःस्थलाद् वने वासो न्यासः शीर्षणि संस्थितः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhāśramo jaghanato brahmacaryaṃ hṛdo mama vakṣaḥsthalād vane vāso nyāsaḥ śīrṣaṇi saṃsthitaḥ
translation: The order of householder originated from my loins, and that of life-long celibates from my heart, the order of forest-dwelling hermits from my chest and the order of Sannyāsins from the crown of my head.

devanagari: वर्णानां आश्रमाणां च जन्मभूम्यनुसारिणीः । आसन् प्रकृतयो नॄणां नीचैः नीचोत्तमोत्तमाः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: varṇānāṃ āśramāṇāṃ ca janmabhūmyanusāriṇīḥ āsan prakṛtayo nṝṇāṃ nīcaiḥ nīcottamottamāḥ
translation: The natures of persons belonging to the different classes and orders of society follow the source of the limbs (of the Cosmic Man) from which they are evolved. The lower the limb (as source of evolution), the lower the nature of activities, the higher the member of the body (as the source), the nobler the activities.

devanagari: शमो दमस्तपः शौचं संतोषः क्षांतिरार्जवम् । मद्‍भक्तिश्च दया सत्यं ब्रह्मप्रकृतयस्त्विमाः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: śamo damastapaḥ śaucaṃ saṃtoṣaḥ kṣāṃtirārjavam mad‍bhaktiśca dayā satyaṃ brahmaprakṛtayastvimāḥ
translation: Control over the mind, restraint over senses, austerities, (physical and mental) purity, contentment, forbearance, straightforwardness, devotion to me, mercifulness and truthfulness are the natural qualities of Brāhmaṇas.

devanagari: तेजो बलं धृतिः शौर्यं तितिक्षौदार्यमुद्यमः । स्थैर्यं ब्रह्मण्यतैश्वर्यं क्षत्र प्रकृतयस्त्विमाः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tejo balaṃ dhṛtiḥ śauryaṃ titikṣaudāryamudyamaḥ sthairyaṃ brahmaṇyataiśvaryaṃ kṣatra prakṛtayastvimāḥ
translation: Majesty, strength, courage, valour, endurance, liberlity, industriousness, firmness, friendliness to Brāhmaṇas—these are natural qualities of Kṣattriyas.

devanagari: आस्तिक्यं दाननिष्ठा च अदंभो ब्रह्मसेवनम् । अतुष्टिः अर्थोपचयैः वैश्य प्रकृतयस्त्विमाः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: āstikyaṃ dānaniṣṭhā ca adaṃbho brahmasevanam atuṣṭiḥ arthopacayaiḥ vaiśya prakṛtayastvimāḥ
translation: Faith (in Vedas and preceptors), firm adherence to liberality, absence of hypocrisy, rendering service to Brāhmaṇas dissatisfaction with amassed wealth (urge to acquire more)—these are the characteristics of the Vaiśyas.

devanagari: शुश्रूषणं द्विजगवां देवानां चापि अमायया । तत्र लब्धेन संतोषः शूद्र प्रकृतयस्त्विमाः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: śuśrūṣaṇaṃ dvijagavāṃ devānāṃ cāpi amāyayā tatra labdhena saṃtoṣaḥ śūdra prakṛtayastvimāḥ
translation: Rendering sincere service to twice-born castes (Brāhmaṇas, Kṣattriyas and Vaiśyas), cows and gods and satisfaction with what one gets therein are the natural characteristics of Śūdras.

devanagari: अशौचमनृतं स्तेयं नास्तिक्यं शुष्कविग्रहः । कामः क्रोधश्च तर्षश्च स्वभावोन्त्यावसायिनाम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: aśaucamanṛtaṃ steyaṃ nāstikyaṃ śuṣkavigrahaḥ kāmaḥ krodhaśca tarṣaśca svabhāvontyāvasāyinām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ stai class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ stai class 1 verb]

translation: Absence of cleanliness, falsehood, thieving, heterodoxy or want of faith, quarrelsomeness (without a proper cause) strong lust and violent anger and inordinate covetiousness—these are found in the nature of the lowest castes.

devanagari: अहिंसा सत्यमस्तेयं अकामक्रोधलोभता । भूतप्रियहितेहा च धर्मोऽयं सार्ववर्णिकः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: ahiṃsā satyamasteyaṃ akāmakrodhalobhatā bhūtapriyahitehā ca dharmo
translation: Non-violence (in thought, word and deed) truthfulness, abstention from taking what is not given, freedom from lust, anger and greed, desire to work for the happiness and welfare of all beings—these constitute the righteous duties of all classes of society.

devanagari: द्वितीयं प्राप्यानुपूर्व्यात् जन्मोपानयनं द्विजः । वसन् गुरुकुले दान्तो ब्रह्माधीयीत चाहूतः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: dvitīyaṃ prāpyānupūrvyāt janmopānayanaṃ dvijaḥ vasan gurukule dānto brahmādhīyīta cāhūtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vas class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vas class 1 verb]

translation: Having undergone purificatory rites since his conception and having obtained the second birth through the investiture of the sacred thread (upanayana), the twice-born boy should control his senses and reside in the house of his preceptor and recite the Vedas, study the interpretation thereof as per instructions of the preceptor.

devanagari: मेखला अजिन दण्डाक्ष ब्रह्मसूत्रकमण्डलून् । जटिलो अधौतदद्वासः अरक्तपीठः कुशान् दधत् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: mekhalā ajina daṇḍākṣa brahmasūtrakamaṇḍalūn jaṭilo adhautadadvāsaḥ araktapīṭhaḥ kuśān dadhat
translation: (During celibacy) he should wear a girdle of the sacred (Muñja) grass (round his waist), a deer-skin, and have a staff, a string of beads, the sacred thread and a jar of water (made of gourd) and matted locks of hair; he should not brush his teeth nor use clothes washed at a laundry). He should not use a painted seat but should hold sacred grass in his hands (as a symbol of Purity and humble approach to the teacher).

devanagari: स्नानभोजनहोमेषु जपोच्चारे च वाग्यतः । न च्छिंद्यान् नखरोमाणि कक्षउपस्थगतान्यपि ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: snānabhojanahomeṣu japoccāre ca vāgyataḥ na cchiṃdyān nakharomāṇi kakṣaupasthagatānyapi
translation: He should observe silence at the time of bath, taking meals, offering oblations to the sacred fire, repeating sacred syllables (japa) and at the time of excretion. He should not pare his nails nor remove the hair from the armpits and secret parts of his body.

devanagari: रेतो न अवकिरेत् जातु ब्रह्मव्रतधरः स्वयम् । अवकीर्णे अवगाह्य अप्सु यतासुः त्रिपदीं जपेत् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: reto na avakiret jātu brahmavratadharaḥ svayam avakīrṇe avagāhya apsu yatāsuḥ tripadīṃ japet

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]
[imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: He should observe strict celibacy and should not voluntarily allow his semen to escape. In case of involuntary escape (as in wet-dreams), he should take bath, perform breath control and repeat the Gāyatrī mantra.

devanagari: अग्न्यर्काचार्यगोविप्र गुरुवृद्धसुरान् शुचिः । समाहित उपासीत संध्ये च यतवाग्जपन् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: agnyarkācāryagovipra guruvṛddhasurān śuciḥ samāhita upāsīta saṃdhye ca yatavāgjapan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jap class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ jap class 1 verb]

translation: Pure of body and with concentrated mind, he should silently repeat the Gāyatrī mantra (while performing sandhya) both in the morning and in the evening[4] and should worship the Fire god (by offering oblations), the Sun-god (by performing sandhya) and other gods and wait upon the preceptor (who initiated him with the Gāyatrī-mantra), the cows, Brāhmaṇas, elderly men.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Silence need not necessarily be observed during the sandhyā-performance at noon Bhāvāratha Dīpikā EK.

devanagari: आचार्यं मां विजानीयात् नअवमन्येत कर्हिचित् । न मर्त्यबुद्ध्यासूयेत सर्वदेवमयो गुरुः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: ācāryaṃ māṃ vijānīyāt naavamanyeta karhicit na martyabuddhyāsūyeta sarvadevamayo guruḥ
translation: He should look upon his preceptor as myself (god himself) and should never disrespect him in any way. He should not peep into his weak points considering him an ordinary mortal, for the preceptor represents all gods in himself.

devanagari: सायं प्रातः उपानीय भैक्ष्यं तस्मै निवेदयेत् । यच्चान्यद् अपि अनुज्ञातं उपयुञ्जीत संयतः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: sāyaṃ prātaḥ upānīya bhaikṣyaṃ tasmai nivedayet yaccānyad api anujñātaṃ upayuñjīta saṃyataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sā class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ sā class 4 verb]
[accusative plural from √ prā class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ prā class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ prā class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: He should offer to his preceptor whatever alms and anything else he gets, both in the morning and in the evening. Observing self-control, he should use only what is permitted by his teacher.

devanagari: शुश्रूषमाण आचार्यं सदाउपासीत नीचवत् । यान शय्यासनस्थानैः नातिदूरे कृताञ्जलिः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: śuśrūṣamāṇa ācāryaṃ sadāupāsīta nīcavat yāna śayyāsanasthānaiḥ nātidūre kṛtāñjaliḥ
translation: While rendering service to the preceptor, he (the pupil) should wait upon him with humility (as one of his subordinates), (following him) when he goes out, (resting vigilantly) while he sleeps,[5] (shampooing his feet) when he sits to rest, standing nearby with folded palms when he sits.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
When the teacher is in bed, the pupil should not be there but rest vigilantly somewhere nearby to be able to attend if called.—EB. 17.320.

devanagari: एवंवृत्तो गुरुकुले वसेद् ‍भोगविवर्जितः । विद्या समाप्यते यावद् बिभ्रद् व्रतं अखण्डितम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃvṛtto gurukule vased ‍bhogavivarjitaḥ vidyā samāpyate yāvad bibhrad vrataṃ akhaṇḍitam
translation: Behaving himself in this manner and observing strictly his vow of celibacy, he should live in the preceptor’s house without indulging into pleasure, till his course of studies is completed.

devanagari: यदि असौ छंदसां लोकं आरोक्ष्यन् ब्रह्मविष्टपम् । गुरवे विन्यसेद् देहं स्वाध्यायार्थं बृहद्व्रतः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: yadi asau chaṃdasāṃ lokaṃ ārokṣyan brahmaviṣṭapam gurave vinyased dehaṃ svādhyāyārthaṃ bṛhadvrataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: If he is desirous of attaining Maharloka and the Brahmaloka (or god Brahmā’s region where Vedas attend in person as stated in verse 5 above), he should adopt the vow of life-long celibacy and dedicate his person[6] to his preceptor for advanced course of studies.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
With a view to repay the debt of the preceptor—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: अग्नौ गुरावात्मनि च सर्वभूतेषु मां परम् । अपृथग्धीः उपसीत ब्रह्मवर्चस्वी अकल्मषः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: agnau gurāvātmani ca sarvabhūteṣu māṃ param apṛthagdhīḥ upasīta brahmavarcasvī akalmaṣaḥ
translation: Resplendent with Vedic lustre and cleansed of all sins, he should entertain the notion of non-ḍifference (with the universe) and worship me, the Supreme Lord in the sacrificial fire, the preceptor, himself and in all beings.

devanagari: स्त्रीणां निरीक्षण स्पर्श संलाप क्ष्वेलनादिकम् । प्राणिनो मिथुनीभूतान् अगृहस्थो अग्रतस्त्यजेत् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: strīṇāṃ nirīkṣaṇa sparśa saṃlāpa kṣvelanādikam prāṇino mithunībhūtān agṛhastho agratastyajet
translation: A person who is not a householder (i.e. a celibate or a recluse) should scrupulously avoid (lustful) look, touch, conversation of jokes and such other contacts with women. Nay he should avoid the sight to a sexual intercourse of animals.

devanagari: शौचं आचमनं स्नानं संध्योपासनमार्जवम् । तीर्थसेवा जपोऽस्पृश्या अभक्ष्य संभाष्यवर्जनम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: śaucaṃ ācamanaṃ snānaṃ saṃdhyopāsanamārjavam tīrthasevā japo
translation: (Physical and mental) purity, Ācamana (sipping water before beginning a sacred rite, etc.) bathing, offering twilight-prayers (to the sun both in the morning and in the evening), straight-forwardness, visiting sacred places, repeating (silently) sacred syllables (mantras), abstention from touching the untouchable, from eating the forbidden articles of food and from speaking with the un-speechworthy, firm faith that I abide in all beings and control over the mind, speech and the body—This rule of discipline[7] is enjoined to all stages of life (Āśrama), O delight of your family.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
EK takes the last two, viz. Faith in the immanence of Brahman in all beings and control over mind, speech and the body as common to all Varṇas and Āśramas.

devanagari: सर्वाश्रमप्रयुक्तोऽयं नियमः कुलनंदन । मद्‍भवः सर्वभूतेषु मनोवाक्कायसंयमः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvāśramaprayukto mad‍bhavaḥ sarvabhūteṣu manovākkāyasaṃyamaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first dual], [perfect active second plural]
[perfect active first dual], [perfect active second plural]
[perfect active first dual], [perfect active second plural]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: (Physical and mental) purity, Ācamana (sipping water before beginning a sacred rite, etc.) bathing, offering twilight-prayers (to the sun both in the morning and in the evening), straight-forwardness, visiting sacred places, repeating (silently) sacred syllables (mantras), abstention from touching the untouchable, from eating the forbidden articles of food and from speaking with the un-speechworthy, firm faith that I abide in all beings and control over the mind, speech and the body—This rule of discipline[7] is enjoined to all stages of life (Āśrama), O delight of your family.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
EK takes the last two, viz. Faith in the immanence of Brahman in all beings and control over mind, speech and the body as common to all Varṇas and Āśramas.

devanagari: एवं बृहद्व्रतधरो ब्राह्मणोऽग्निः इव ज्वलन् । मद्‍भक्तः तीव्रतपसा दग्धकर्माशयोऽमलः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ bṛhadvratadharo brāhmaṇo mad‍bhaktaḥ tīvratapasā dagdhakarmāśayo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jval class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ jval class 1 verb]

translation: In this way, the life-long celibate (Brāhmaṇa) who has burnt down all the seeds of Karmas by his rigorously austere penance, and had become absolutely pure and is devoted to me becomes resplendent like fire.

devanagari: अथ अनंतरं आवेक्ष्यन् यथा जिज्ञासितागमः । गुरवे दक्षिणां दत्त्वा स्नायाद् गुर्वनुमोदितः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: atha anaṃtaraṃ āvekṣyan yathā jijñāsitāgamaḥ gurave dakṣiṇāṃ dattvā snāyād gurvanumoditaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[imperfect middle first single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ gurv class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ gurv class 1 verb]

translation: If a celibate student who has completed the study and investigation of the Śāstras (according to his need in life), wishes to enter the next stage of life viz. that of a householder, he should pay the educational fees to his preceptor and with, the permission of his preceptor, he should take the bath (for terminating his celibate stage, technically known as the Samāvartana saṃskāra).

devanagari: गृहं वनं वोपविशेत् प्रव्रजे द्वा द्विजोत्तमः । आश्रमादाश्रमं गच्छेत् नान्यथा मत्परश्चरेत् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhaṃ vanaṃ vopaviśet pravraje dvā dvijottamaḥ āśramādāśramaṃ gacchet nānyathā matparaścaret
translation: He should enter the house (i.e. householder’s stage of life, if sexually inclined) or the forest (i.e. the life of a Vānaprastha, if he desires to purify his mind). If that excellent Brāhmaṇa is already of pure heart, he should renounce the world (and become a sannyāsin). Or (if he so pleases) he may pass from one stage of life to another but never in the reverse order e.g. from Sannyāsa to Vānaprastha[8] etc. (The āśramic order is not binding on my devotees).
purport:
Footnote [8]:
Unless a person is absolutely devoted to me, the āśramic order is binding on him. This rule is not binding on my devotees (Bhāvāratha Dīpikā).

devanagari: गृहार्थी सदृशीं भार्यां उद्वहेद् अजुगुप्सिताम् । यवीयसीं तु वयसा यां सवर्णां अनुक्रमात् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhārthī sadṛśīṃ bhāryāṃ udvahed ajugupsitām yavīyasīṃ tu vayasā yāṃ savarṇāṃ anukramāt
translation:
A person wishing to enter the householder’s stage should espouse a befitting girl, with no defect (in heredity, habits or character), junior to him in age and belonging to his own caste (class, varṇas).
(It is only after marrying the girl of his own caste if he be sexually unsatisfied) he should take a girl from a class in the prescribed serial order.[9]
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā quotes a Smṛti which allows the anuloma order in marriage: Thus a Brāhmaṇa can marry a girl from Kṣattriya, Vaiśya and Śūdra classes; a Kṣattriya from Vaiśya and Śūdra varṇas and a Śūdra can marry only a Śūdra girl.

devanagari: इज्यअध्ययनदानानि सर्वेषां च द्विजन्मनाम् । प्रतिग्रहोअध्यापनं च ब्राह्मणस्यैव याजनम् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: ijyaadhyayanadānāni sarveṣāṃ ca dvijanmanām pratigrahoadhyāpanaṃ ca brāhmaṇasyaiva yājanam
translation: (The duties of) performance of sacrifices, Vedic study and conferring charitable gifts are binding on all twice- born classes (viz. Brāhmaṇa, Kṣattriya and Vaiśya) but acceptance of gifts, teaching of Vedas and (actual) performance of sacrifices are the (additional) duties of Brāhmaṇas.

devanagari: प्रतिग्रहं मन्यमानः तपस्तेजोयशोनुदम् । अन्याभ्यामेव जीवेत शिलैर्वा दोषदृक् तयोः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: pratigrahaṃ manyamānaḥ tapastejoyaśonudam anyābhyāmeva jīveta śilairvā doṣadṛk tayoḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: A Brāhmaṇa who considers that acceptance of gifts from others is detrimental to his austerities, Brahmanical (i.e. spiritual) power and glory, should live by the other two means of livelihood (viz. teaching the Vedas and acting as a sacrificial priest). He who feels these two vocations as detracting (to his spiritual position or power,) should live by gleaning grains of corn from the field (when the owner has taken away the produce).

devanagari: ब्राह्मणस्य हि देहोऽयं क्षुद्रकामाय नेष्यते । कृच्छ्राय तपसे चेह प्रेत्यानन्तसुखाय च ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: brāhmaṇasya hi deho kṛcchrāya tapase ceha pretyānantasukhāya ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: This body of a Brāhmaṇa is not certainly meant for trivial enjoyments. It is intended for undergoing distress and austere penance in this world, and infinite bliss (of Mokṣa) after death.

devanagari: शिलोञ्छवृत्त्या परितुष्टचित्तो । धर्मं महांतं विरजं जुषाणः । मय्यर्पितात्मा गृह एव तिष्ठन् । नातिप्रसक्तः समुपैति शांतिम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: śiloñchavṛttyā parituṣṭacitto dharmaṃ mahāṃtaṃ virajaṃ juṣāṇaḥ mayyarpitātmā gṛha eva tiṣṭhan nātiprasaktaḥ samupaiti śāṃtim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation: He whose mind is content with (what he gets by) gleaning grains of corn (from the fields after the owner had reaped and taken home the food-grains and from the marketplace when the market day is over) and who observes the pure and noble duty of offering hospitality to guests and has dedicated his heart to me and is not much addicted to his house (though himself a householder) attains to eternal spiritual peace.

devanagari: समुद्धरंति ये विप्रं सीदंतं मत्परायणम् । तान् उद्धरिष्ये न चिराद् आपद्‍भ्यो नौः इवार्णवात् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: samuddharaṃti ye vipraṃ sīdaṃtaṃ matparāyaṇam tān uddhariṣye na cirād āpad‍bhyo nauḥ ivārṇavāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ hṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Like unto a boat saving a man drowning into a sea, I promptly save from calamities those persons who redeem a Brāhmaṇa devoted to me or as a matter of that anyone who has dedicated himself to me but is undergoing hardships.

devanagari: सर्वाः समुद्धरेद् राजा पितेव व्यसनात् प्रजाः । आत्मानं आत्मना धीरो यथा गजपतिर्गजान् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvāḥ samuddhared rājā piteva vyasanāt prajāḥ ātmānaṃ ātmanā dhīro yathā gajapatirgajān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle third single], [imperative active second plural]

translation: Like a father protecting his children from difficulties a king should defend his subjects against all calamities. Just as a king-elephant lifts out (of mire) other elephants of his group and comes out of it by dint of his own power, the wise and courageous king should (after redeeming his subject) extricate himself out of the difficulty by his own efforts.

devanagari: एवंविधो नरपतिः विमानेनार्कवर्चसा । विधूय इह अशुभं कृत्स्नं इंद्रेण सह मोदते ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃvidho narapatiḥ vimānenārkavarcasā vidhūya iha aśubhaṃ kṛtsnaṃ iṃdreṇa saha modate
translation: A king who discharges his duties in this manner, shakes off in this very life, whatever inauspiciousness and evil (that has clung to him), by the merit accruing to him by his just rule over his kingdom and in a celestial car brilliant like the sun, he goes to heaven and enjoys heavenly pleasures in the company of Indra (king of gods).

devanagari: सीदन् विप्रः वणिक् वृत्त्या पण्यैः एवापदं तरेत् । खड्गेन वा आपदाक्रांतो न श्ववृत्त्या कथञ्चन ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: sīdan vipraḥ vaṇik vṛttyā paṇyaiḥ evāpadaṃ taret khaḍgena vā āpadākrāṃto na śvavṛttyā kathañcana

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sad class 1 verb]

translation: In times of adversity, a Brāhmaṇa should take to the commercial profession and should surmount the difficult times by selling non-prohibited articles.[10] If, however, he still gets into difficulties (by suffering a loss in his business), he should make his way through that emergency by taking to the sword,[11] but he should never resort to a dog’s way of life (by undertaking lower jobs).
purport:
Footnote [10]:
A Brāhmaṇa is not allowed to sell articles like salt, wine etc. (by Dharmaśāstra)—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā To this list EK adds—Ghee, oil, sesamum, cotton (EB. 17.457).

Footnote [11]:
Gautamasmṛti regards a soldier’s profession superior to that of a merchant but soldierly profession involves hiṃsā (injury to life). Hence Lord’s preference to the Vaiśya way of life—SR.

devanagari: वैश्यवृत्त्या तु राजन्यो जीवेत् मृगययापदि । चरेद् वा विप्ररूपेण न श्ववृत्त्या कथञ्चन ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: vaiśyavṛttyā tu rājanyo jīvet mṛgayayāpadi cared vā viprarūpeṇa na śvavṛttyā kathañcana
translation: During distress, a Kṣattriya should adopt the profession of a Vaiśya or live by hunting or should take to livelihood of a Brāhmaṇa (by accepting a teaching job, but he should never take to the life of a Śūdra.

devanagari: शूद्रवृत्तिं भजेद् वैश्यः शूद्रः कारुकटक्रियाम् । कृच्छ्रान् मुक्तो न गर्ह्येण वृत्तिं लिप्सेत कर्मणा ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: śūdravṛttiṃ bhajed vaiśyaḥ śūdraḥ kārukaṭakriyām kṛcchrān mukto na garhyeṇa vṛttiṃ lipseta karmaṇā
translation: Under adverse circumstance, a person from Vaiśya community should follow the vocations of a Śūdra-artisans like mat-weaving. But once he had overcome the difficulty, he should not hanker after the censurable calling (recommended by Dharmaśāstra to the lower strata of society).

devanagari: वेदाध्याय स्वधा स्वाहा बलि अन्नाद्यैः यथोदयम् । देवर्षिपितृभूतानि मद् रूपाणि अन्वहं यजेत् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: vedādhyāya svadhā svāhā bali annādyaiḥ yathodayam devarṣipitṛbhūtāni mad rūpāṇi anvahaṃ yajet
translation: A house-holder should according to his financial position, look upon gods, sages, manes and other beings as my embodiments and every day worship them by offering oblations in sacrificial fires uttering the word svāhā at every oblation to a particular deity in the case of gods. Study and recitation of Vedas (technically known as Brahma-yajña, meant for sages), oblations (of water known as Tarpaṇa and Śrāddha for departed ancestors) accompanied with the pronunciations of the word svadhā, offering of food (bali) to living beings and serving of food to guests (known as Manuṣya-yajña.

devanagari: यदृच्छया उपपन्नेन शुक्लेन उपार्जितेन वा । धनेन अपीडयन् भृत्यान् न्यायेन एव आहरेत् क्रतून् ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: yadṛcchayā upapannena śuklena upārjitena vā dhanena apīḍayan bhṛtyān nyāyena eva āharet kratūn
translation: Without causing any hardship to his dependants (wife, children, servant, etc.), one should perform sacrifices as per proper rites and formalities with the wealth got without any efforts (as by legal inheritance) or with money earned by following one’s profession or with the wealth obtained by fair means.

devanagari: कुटुंबेषु न सज्जेत न प्रमाद्येत् कुटुंबी अपि । विपश्चित् नश्वरं पश्येद् अदृष्टमपि दृष्टवत् ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: kuṭuṃbeṣu na sajjeta na pramādyet kuṭuṃbī api vipaścit naśvaraṃ paśyed adṛṣṭamapi dṛṣṭavat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]
[nominative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb]

translation: One should not entertain attachment to the members of one’s family. Even though one be a householder, he should not be negligent in his devotion to Goḍ. An intelligent person should realize that the unseen enjoyments in heaven (promised by the Pūrva Mīmāṃsā) are as momentary as the visible world.

devanagari: पुत्रदारा आप्तबंधूनां संगमः पांथसङ्गमः । अनुदेहं वियन्त्येते स्वप्नो निद्रानुगो यथा ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: putradārā āptabaṃdhūnāṃ saṃgamaḥ pāṃthasaṅgamaḥ anudehaṃ viyantyete svapno nidrānugo yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active second plural], [aorist middle third single]
[nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]

[nominative dual from √ vī class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ vī class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ vī class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ vī class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ vī class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ vī class 2 verb]

translation: Companionship and association with one’s sons, wives, kith and kin is momentary like the get-together of travellers (for drinking water). With the change of one’s body (in the next birth), they part company like a dream that disappears with the sleep.

devanagari: इत्थं परिमृशन् मुक्तो गृहेषु अतिथिवद् वसन् । न गृहैः अनुबध्येत निर्ममो निरहङ्कृतः ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ parimṛśan mukto gṛheṣu atithivad vasan na gṛhaiḥ anubadhyeta nirmamo nirahaṅkṛtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ mṛś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ mṛś class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ mṛś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ mṛś class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ mṛś class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ vas class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vas class 1 verb]

translation: Having thus realized the truth after deep thought, and staying in his house (unconcerned) like a stranger or a guest, he, being devoid of the notion of ‘I-ness’ and ‘mine-ness’ (with reference to his body, relatives and property) is not bound by (and attached to) his household property and leads the life of one liberated while alive.[12]
purport:
Footnote [12]:
Siddhāntapradīpa ‘free from desires and attachment’
EK. ‘Is automatically liberated even as a householder’ Bhāgavata Candrikā, Padaratnāvalī, Bhaktamanorañjanī read yukta for mukta and interpret as ‘one who has concentrated his mind in me (samāhita).

devanagari: कर्मभिः गृहमेधीयैः इष्ट्वा मामेव भक्तिमान् । तिष्ठेद् वनं वोपविशेत् प्रजावान् वा परिव्रजेत् ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: karmabhiḥ gṛhamedhīyaiḥ iṣṭvā māmeva bhaktimān tiṣṭhed vanaṃ vopaviśet prajāvān vā parivrajet
translation: Having propitiated me alone through devout worship in the form of performance of duties prescribed for a house-holder, my devotee may continue to stay at home (as a house-holder) or live in a forest as an anchorite (Vānaprastha) or become a recluse (sannyāsin) if he has got children (and a continuation of his race).

devanagari: यस्तु आसक्तमतिः गेहे पुत्रवित्तैषणा आतुरः । स्त्रैणः कृपणधीः मूढो मम अहं इति बध्यते ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: yastu āsaktamatiḥ gehe putravittaiṣaṇā āturaḥ straiṇaḥ kṛpaṇadhīḥ mūḍho mama ahaṃ iti badhyate
translation: But he whose heart is deeply attached to his household and eagerly pines for getting money and children and is obsessed with sex, is a man of poor understanding. Such a fool is bound with the notions of ‘I-ness’ and ‘mine-ness’.

devanagari: अहो मे पितरौ वृद्धौ भार्या बालात्मजऽत्मजाः । अनाथा मामृते दीनाः कथं जीवन्ति दुःखिताः ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: aho me pitarau vṛddhau bhāryā bālātmaja anāthā māmṛte dīnāḥ kathaṃ jīvanti duḥkhitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vṛdh class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ jīv class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ jīv class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ jīv class 1 verb]

translation: “Alas! My parents are aged. My wife and my young children—all of them have no protector without me! How can those helpless and miserable persons live without me?”

devanagari: एवं गृहाशयाक्षिप्त हृदयो मूढधीः अयम् । अतृप्तस्तान् अनुध्यायन् मृतोऽन्धं विशते तमः ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ gṛhāśayākṣipta hṛdayo mūḍhadhīḥ ayam atṛptastān anudhyāyan mṛto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ mṛ class 6 verb]

translation:
With his heart thus overwhelmed and distracted with anxious attachment to his house, this dull-witted fellow remains dissatisfied and dies while brooding over them and enters the blinding darkness (of hell after death).
